cases.title: Participant first registration for an event succeeds with single active row
preconditions: Event is in RegistrationOpen state with available seats
Registration window is active per BR-02
Participant has no prior registration for the event
PostgreSQL test database with registration module transaction boundaries
steps: Invoke registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext) within a real DB transaction
Query registrations for participantId and eventId with active states
cases.expected: Registration succeeds with state Registered (or Waitlisted if full per capacity rules); exactly one active registration row exists for the participant/event pair; FR-06 satisfied for initial registration

cases.title: POST registrations succeeds once for authenticated participant
preconditions: Published event with registration opened and available capacity
Participant JWT with no existing registration for the event
Fresh Idempotency-Key header
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Participant Authorization bearer
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status with same token
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with registrationId, state Registered, and participantId matching token; registration-status returns the same single active registration; participant has successfully registered once for the event

cases.title: Duplicate registration attempt blocked per testing-plan §3
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with available seats and active registration window
Participant already has active Registered registration for the event
steps: Invoke registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext) a second time for the same participant
Observe thrown error and count active registration rows
cases.expected: Registration rejected with ApiError code REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE and HTTP status 409; no second registration row created; existing registration state unchanged; testing-plan §3 row 'Duplicate registration attempt → blocked' satisfied

cases.title: Duplicate POST registrations returns 409 with typed error envelope
preconditions: Participant successfully POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations once
Fresh Idempotency-Key header for duplicate attempt
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with same participant Authorization bearer a second time
Parse error response body and re-fetch registration-status
cases.expected: HTTP 409 with error.code REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE, user-safe message per API error contract, requestId, and details; GET registration-status still returns original registration unchanged; participant cannot register more than once per event

cases.title: Participant UI prevents duplicate registration with clear feedback
preconditions: Participant logged in and already Registered for an open event
Event detail page shows current Registered status badge or label
steps: Navigate to event detail for the event where participant is already registered
Attempt to register again via UI (register button, retry, or equivalent action if exposed)
Observe inline or toast error messaging and CTA state
cases.expected: UI does not create a second registration; participant sees clear duplicate-registration message (inline error, disabled CTA, or toast) consistent with server REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE semantics

cases.title: Duplicate blocked when participant is already Waitlisted
preconditions: Event is full with waitlistEnabled true
Participant already has active Waitlisted registration for the event
Active WaitlistEntry exists for the registration (BR-04b: promoted_at and expired_at both null)
steps: Attempt registrationService.register for the same participant and event again
cases.expected: Rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE (409); waitlistPosition and single Waitlisted row unchanged; no second waitlist entry; active WaitlistEntry row count remains 1; at-most-once applies to all active states not only Registered

cases.title: Duplicate blocked when participant is already CheckedIn
preconditions: Participant has active registration in CheckedIn state for the event
Event registration window still open if applicable
steps: Attempt registrationService.register for the same participant and event
cases.expected: Rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; CheckedIn registration remains the sole active record; participant cannot register again while CheckedIn is active

cases.title: Re-registration allowed after valid cancellation releases inactive state
preconditions: Participant previously Registered then cancelled (CancelledByUser) before deadline per BR-07
Event RegistrationOpen with available seats
No other active registration for participant on this event
steps: Invoke registrationService.register for the same participant after cancellation completed
Query active registration for participant/event pair
cases.expected: New registration succeeds with state Registered; FR-06 satisfied (at most one active registration at a time) because cancelled state is not active; prior cancelled row does not count as duplicate

cases.title: Concurrent duplicate registration requests from same participant yield single active row
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with available seats
Same participantId used in parallel registrationService.register calls with no prior registration
steps: Fire two or more concurrent register calls for the same participantId and eventId
Count active registration rows for the participant/event pair
cases.expected: Exactly one request succeeds creating an active registration; all other concurrent attempts rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE or equivalent conflict; database never holds more than one active registration per participant/event; transactional consistency per API concurrency guardrails

cases.title: Duplicate POST with new Idempotency-Key still rejected
preconditions: Participant successfully registered via POST with Idempotency-Key K1
Second POST prepared with different Idempotency-Key K2
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Idempotency-Key K2 for the same authenticated participant
cases.expected: HTTP 409 with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; deduplication guard applies independently of idempotency key reuse; FR-06 is not bypassed by retry headers

cases.title: Same participant may register for different events without duplicate conflict
preconditions: Two distinct events EventA and EventB both RegistrationOpen with available seats
Participant registered for EventA only
steps: Invoke registrationService.register for EventB with same participantId
Verify both event registrations exist independently
cases.expected: Registration for EventB succeeds; FR-06 scope is per event not global; participant has one active registration per event with no cross-event duplicate rejection

cases.title: acceptRegistration state transition guard rejects when duplicate active registration exists
preconditions: Registration state machine transition acceptRegistration invoked
Participant already has active registration row for event
steps: Attempt acceptRegistration transition for duplicate participant/event pair
Inspect error and preserved registration state
cases.expected: Transition rejected with 409 Conflict and stable error code; original state unchanged per invalid transition policy; state machine guard requires window open + no duplicate + seat available

cases.title: Dedupe validation runs before capacity assignment in registration pipeline
preconditions: Event full with waitlist disabled (would otherwise reject with REGISTRATION_REJECTED_FULL)
Participant already has active Registered registration
steps: Attempt second registration for same participant
Inspect error code on failure
cases.expected: Failure code is REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE not capacity/full rejection; confirms pipeline step 3 (dedup active registration) executes before step 4 (capacity decision) per validation pipeline

cases.title: P0 invariant: at most one active registration per participant per event in persistence
preconditions: Database with registration module constraints enabled including uq_active_registration partial index
Any sequence of registration attempts including retries and concurrent calls for same participant/event
steps: After exercising duplicate-block scenarios, query registrations for participantId and eventId where state is active (Requested, Registered, Waitlisted, CheckedIn)
cases.expected: Row count for active states never exceeds 1; aligns with testing plan P0 severity for duplicate active registration defects and system overview consistency invariant

cases.title: Database partial unique index prevents duplicate active registration at persistence layer
preconditions: PostgreSQL with uq_active_registration partial index on (event_id, participant_id) for active states (Requested, Registered, Waitlisted, CheckedIn)
One active Registered row already persisted for participant/event
steps: Attempt direct insert or race-condition bypass of second active registration row at DB layer
Observe constraint violation
cases.expected: Second active row insert fails with unique constraint violation; persistence layer enforces FR-06 even if application guard is bypassed; defense in depth per database design unique active registration index

cases.title: Flow B duplicate registration step blocked after waitlist entry
preconditions: E2E Flow B fixture: participant already Waitlisted on a full event with active WaitlistEntry (BR-04b)
Valid participant JWT and fresh idempotency key
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations again for the waitlisted participant
Verify error response and GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status before proceeding with cancellation/promotion steps
cases.expected: HTTP 409 with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; original Waitlisted registration unchanged with same registrationId and waitlistPosition; active WaitlistEntry row count remains 1; core scenario matrix duplicate registration attempt blocked

cases.title: OrganizerAdmin and OrganizerStaff denied POST registrations (cannot trigger duplicate path)
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with available seats
Valid JWT for OrganizerAdmin (or OrganizerStaff scoped to the event)
Actor has no participant registration for the event
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with OrganizerAdmin bearer token and Idempotency-Key
Repeat POST with OrganizerStaff bearer token for the same event
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 403 unauthorized; no registration row is created; Register for event is Participant-only per roles-permissions matrix — duplicate prevention is not reachable by non-participant roles
